title: Lamb-Herb Canadian Stew
description: A hearty, one-pot stew featuring lamb, herbs, and chocolate chips, all while staying within the Whole30 guidelines. This dish is perfect for a cold winter's night.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat the oil over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb and cook until browned on all sides, about 2 minutes per side.
Remove the lamb and set it aside, then add the onion and garlic to the pot. Cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the thyme and rosemary to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Return the lamb to the pot, then add the broth and chocolate chips. Stir to combine, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the lamb is tender, about 2 hours.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly, then stir in the fresh herbs. Serve hot, garnished with additional herbs if desired.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the stew over cauliflower mash or with a side of roasted vegetables for a filling meal.
